testing-object-state.label: Testing Object State
testing-object-state.summary: Write tests that prove constructors, methods, and state changes work across files.

sketch1.title: Testing Object State: the file shape

sketch2.title: Testing Object State: what the grader checks

try_testing_object_state_sketch0.title: Try it yourself
try_testing_object_state_sketch0.prompt: Complete the test file so it verifies that `deposit()` changes the account balance.

try_testing_object_state_sketch1.title: Try it yourself
try_testing_object_state_sketch1.prompt: Add assertions that prove invalid withdrawals do not change the balance.

ci-test-goal-practice.title: Test a savings goal model
ci-test-goal-practice.prompt: Write assertions for deposit behavior and percent completion.
ci-test-goal-practice.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: In a multi-file project, each file should own one clear responsibility and imports should connect those responsibilities.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

ci-test-goal-practice.starterCode:
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, ".")

from models.savings_goal import SavingsGoal

goal = SavingsGoal("Trip", 200)
# add behavior assertions
print("goal tests passed")

sc-testing-object-state-design.title: Check the design choice
sc-testing-object-state-design.prompt: Which choice best matches the lesson goal for testing object state?
sc-testing-object-state-design.hint: Choose the option that keeps object responsibilities clear.

help.concept: Write tests that prove constructors, methods, and state changes work across files.
help.hint_1: Look for the answer that reduces duplication without hiding behavior.
help.hint_2: A good OOP answer usually keeps behavior near the object that owns the data.

options.a: Put every class in main.py so imports are never needed.
options.b: Give each class or service a focused responsibility and import it where needed.
options.c: Avoid methods so every object exposes all of its data directly.
options.d: Copy the same logic into each subclass to avoid shared code.

try-testing-object-state-sketch0.title: Write a state-change test
try-testing-object-state-sketch0.prompt: Complete the test file so it verifies that `deposit()` changes the account balance.
try-testing-object-state-sketch0.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: In a multi-file project, each file should own one clear responsibility and imports should connect those responsibilities.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

try-testing-object-state-sketch0.starterCode:
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, ".")

from models.account import Account

account = Account("Ava", 10)
# add an assertion after depositing 5
print("account tests passed")

try-testing-object-state-sketch1.title: Test an invalid withdrawal edge case
try-testing-object-state-sketch1.prompt: Add assertions that prove invalid withdrawals do not change the balance.
try-testing-object-state-sketch1.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: In a multi-file project, each file should own one clear responsibility and imports should connect those responsibilities.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

try-testing-object-state-sketch1.starterCode:
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, ".")

from models.wallet import Wallet

wallet = Wallet(20)
# withdraw more than the balance and assert the result
print("wallet tests passed")

concept.title: Testing Object State: the idea
concept.bodyMarkdown: **Testing Object State** is not just syntax. In this course it is practiced inside small projects where one file owns one clear part of the program. Write tests that prove constructors, methods, and state changes work across files.

multifile-shape.title: Testing Object State: the file shape
multifile-shape.bodyMarkdown: The file layout matters. Models hold object state and behavior, services coordinate groups of objects, tests verify behavior, and `main.py` stays thin enough to read.

graded-habit.title: Testing Object State: what the grader checks
graded-habit.bodyMarkdown: The grader checks behavior from the running project. A strong answer keeps imports working, keeps all files present, and proves the object behavior through output or assertions.
